summary: Backend-focused software engineer with 5+ years of experience building scalable distributed systems, automation pipelines, and AI-driven backend services using Python, Java, and TypeScript. Experienced in developing reliable microservices, ML pipelines, and cloud-native infrastructure for data-intensive and healthcare applications.

bullets: Architected and deployed scalable Python backend services and workflow automation pipelines processing 3,300+ healthcare records, enabling automated experimentation, analytics reporting, and reliable execution of ML workflows.

Refactored research prototypes into modular microservices with structured logging, monitoring, and automated tests, improving system performance by 20–30% and increasing reliability of distributed experimentation pipelines.
Designed data validation and feature-processing pipelines supporting end-to-end ML model training, evaluation, and inference workflows across large clinical datasets.
Implemented observability practices, including structured logging and error tracking, improving debugging efficiency and operational visibility for ML experimentation systems.
Built scalable full-stack product features using React, TypeScript, Node.js (Express), and MongoDB, supporting thousands of users in a production UAT environment.
Designed and implemented secure RESTful APIs for identity, authentication, and session management, enabling reliable service integration and audit-friendly request flows.

Implemented CI/CD pipelines using Docker, GitHub Actions, and AWS, integrating automated testing, structured logging, and error tracking to reduce deployment downtime by ~40% and enable faster, safer releases.
Developed Python-based backend components that processed high-volume datasets and supported production web services.
Designed and deployed high-throughput backend services in Java and Python, supporting scalable API-driven data workflows across internal product systems.
Developed RESTful microservices for metadata processing and record linking, improving system scalability and data integration across services.
Optimized PostgreSQL schemas and SQL queries, improving data retrieval performance by ~35% and reducing API latency.
Automated deployments and environment provisioning using Docker, AWS, and CI/CD pipelines, while implementing structured logging and monitoring to improve system reliability and troubleshooting.
Developed scalable full-stack web features using Java (Spring/Grails), Vue.js, and JavaScript, supporting 1,000+ active users in a production SaaS platform.
Implemented secure authentication and authorization workflows using JWT and RBAC, strengthening application security and preventing unauthorized access.
Optimized backend services and PostgreSQL queries through indexing and tuning, reducing p95 API latency by ~20%.
Refactored legacy components into modular services and reusable APIs while contributing to Agile development, code reviews, testing, and production support to deliver stable releases.

projects.name: Fairness-Aware Deep Learning for Visual Field Prediction from OCT
bullets: Designed and trained a 3D deep learning model (3D ResNet-18) to predict 52-point visual field sensitivity from volumetric OCT scans using 3,300+ clinical samples.
Proposed an Adaptive Fairness Feedback (AFF) training strategy to improve subgroup performance and reduce demographic prediction disparities.
Achieved MAE 3.26 dB, RMSE 5.00 dB, and R² = 0.59, with 87% of predictions within ±3 dB of ground truth; manuscript under review at Journal of Medical Imaging (SPIE).

projects.name: Glaucoma Progression Prediction from RNFLT Maps
bullets: Developed deep learning models to predict future glaucoma progression from baseline RNFLT maps using the Harvard-GDP ophthalmology dataset.
Implemented CNN architectures (VGG16-BN, EfficientNet-B0) customized for single-channel OCT inputs (225×225) to perform binary progression classification.
Built training pipelines with class-imbalance handling using weighted BCEWithLogitsLoss and optimized models with AdamW (LR 5×10⁻⁵).

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
education.thesis: Fairness-Aware Deep Learning for Visual Field Prediction from OCT

awards.name: Honors Convocation and Academic Excellence Award
awards.description: Awarded for maintaining a 4.0 GPA throughout graduate studies (2024–2025).
